title: Webflow Subdomain Set Up Guide

intro.text: This guide walks you through connecting your subdomain (e.g. landing.yourcompany.com) to your new Webflow landing page. The whole process takes about 2 minutes and is handled through a tool called Entri, which connects to your DNS provider automatically.
intro.helpText: If you have any troubles, feel free to email Tyce, or book a meeting directly

prerequisites: The subdomain you want to use (e.g. landing, get, go, start, etc.)
Login access to your DNS provider (e.g. Cloudflare, GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains)

steps.label: Step 1
steps.title: Navigate to the publishing area for your Webflow website project
bullets: A gear icon will appear in the top right corner, click it
Then select "Publishing" in the left hand menu bar

steps.label: Step 2
steps.title: Click "add a custom domain" drop down in the "Production" section

steps.label: Step 3
steps.title: The "Entri" modal will pop up. Please click "Continue"

steps.label: Step 4
steps.title: Enter your subdomain and domain
steps.description: Enter "try" in the subdomain field, and your domain (e.g. "yourdomain.com") in the domain field.

steps.label: Step 5
steps.title: Click "Authorize with [Your DNS Provider]"
steps.description: This will ask you to log in to your DNS provider. Then it will add the required records automatically.

steps.label: Step 6 (Final Step)
steps.title: Let us know once it has been done, so we can verify it and complete the final checks
